revision.text:
[[ja:比奈守神社]] {{Expand Japanese|比奈守神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hinamori Shrine | native_name = 比奈守神社 | image = Hinamori jinjya.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hinamori Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.3926667|136.7535306}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین (ایمپیراتور)|bn|সম্রাট ওজিন|cs|Ódžin|de|Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|es|Ōjin Tennō|eu|Ōjin enperadorea|fa|امپراتور اوجین|fi|Ōjin|fr|Ōjin|hr|Ōjin|id|Kaisar Ōjin|it|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|ka|ოძინი|ko|오진 천황|nl|Ojin|pl|Ōjin|pt|Ojin|ru|Император Одзин|sh|Car Ōjin|simple|Emperor Ōjin|sl|Cesar Odžin|sv|Ojin|th|จักรพรรดิโอจิง|tl|Emperador Ōjin|tr|Ōjin (imparator)|uk|Імператор Одзін|uz|Imperator Ōjin|vi|Thiên hoàng Ōjin|yo|Emperor Ōjin|zh_min_nan|Ôzin Thian-hông|zh_yue|應神天皇|zh|应神天皇|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hinamori Shrine'''|比奈守神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11609223|ltq=Q11609223|qid=Q11609223|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mino Province}} ** Series ordinal: 18 ** P155: {{ill|Wakae Shrine|lt=Wakae Shrine|ja|若江神社|qid=Q11616586|1=Wakae Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11617261|ltq=Q11617261|qid=Q11617261|1=Akanabe Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q45798|ltq=Q45798|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} * {{ill|Mino Province|lt=Mino Province|ckb|پارێزگای مینۆ|cs|Mino (provincie)|de|Provinz Mino|en|Mino Province|es|Provincia de Mino|eu|Mino probintzia|fa|ولایت مینو|fr|Province de Mino|gl|Provincia de Mino|hak|Mî-nùng-koet|id|Provinsi Mino|it|Mino (provincia)|ja|美濃国|ko|미노국|lt|Mino žemė|nl|Mino (provincie)|pt|Província de Mino|ru|Мино (провинция)|simple|Mino Province|sk|Mino (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мино|th|แคว้นมิโนะ|tr|Mino (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Міно|vi|Tỉnh Mino|wuu|美濃国|zh_min_nan|Mino-kok|zh_yue|美濃國|zh|美濃國|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} * {{ill|Q11409326|ltq=Q11409326|qid=Q11409326|1=Atsumi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.3926667|136.7535306}} * {{coord|35.3926|136.75354}} == image (P18) == * Hinamori jinjya.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q317997|ltq=Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181940 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひなもりじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005000460 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Hinamorino Shrine|lt=Hinamorino Shrine|qid=Q135039903|1=Hinamorino Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部本郷693-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:比奈守神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11547446}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Hinamori Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Hinamori jinjya.jpg|250px|Hinamori Shrine]]
Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Akanabe Hongō 693-1 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = | 例祭 = April 3 | 神事 = | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Hinamori Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Hinamori Shrine''' (ひなもりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It corresponds to Hinamori Shrine of {{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|qid=Q11409326|1=Atsumi district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. However, there is also a theory that Hinamori Shrine of Atsumi District, Mino Province, is {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Gifu)|Q3517214|qid=Q3517214|lt=Tejikarao Shrine|1=Tejikarao Shrine}} in the same city of Gifu. It is near {{ill|lt=Kami-Akanabe Castle|Q17226097|qid=Q17226097|1=Kamiakanabe Castle}}. === Outline === The time of foundation is unknown. One theory says “Hina” means {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|qid=Q1056535|lt=Hida|1=Hida Province}} or {{ill|lt=Emishi|Q932574|qid=Q932574|lt=barbarian peoples|1=Emishi}}, and that this was a frontier defense point of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato court|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is said to have been a base against attacks by Emishi from Hida, but geographically it lies close (about 1,200 m in a straight line) to the {{ill|lt=Sakaigawa (Gifu)|Q4330698|qid=Q4330698|lt=Sakaigawa River|1=Sakai River (Gifu)}} (the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} before the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}), which was the border with {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari|1=Owari Province}}, so it is also inferred to have been a base against attacks from Owari. It burned down in the {{ill|lt=Funada Battle|Q11614291|qid=Q11614291|1=Battle of Funada}} in {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1495|Q6695|qid=Q6695|1=1495}}). At that time, its founding date and origins became unknown{{r|g171}}. Since nearby residents had also disappeared due to war, it was not rebuilt immediately, and the current shrine buildings were constructed after the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} period. All buildings except the main sanctuary were rebuilt after {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 50. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Hidamori Shrine”{{r|g171}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), the name was restored to “Hinamori Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} *:There are also theories of {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|1=Shitateruhime}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Ōusu|Q11438297|qid=Q11438297|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto}}. === Access === *{{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} Akanabe-Mitahora Line: get off at “Kami-Akanabe” bus stop, 8 minutes on foot **From platform 4 at {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|qid=Q11226229|1=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal}} (north of {{ill|lt=Gifu Station|Q1144885|qid=Q1144885|1=Gifu Station}}), or from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu boarding stop|Q11415771|qid=Q11415771|1=Meitetsu Gifu boarding stop}} (west of {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|Q1109902|qid=Q1109902|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}), take E70 “Shimo-Saba,” E71 “Takakuwa,” or E72 “{{ill|lt=Gifu Transport Bureau|Q11471893|qid=Q11471893|lt=Prefectural Automobile Hall|1=Prefectural Automobile Hall}}” === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs=''Mino no Kuni Shikinaikokushi Genzai Jinja Meisai Torishirabesho'', pp.57-58}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hinamori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hinamori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]
